rows.title: Florida Attorney General Annual Report: 186 Notices of Alleged Violation Issued Under Florida Digital Bill of Rights in 2025
rows.summary:
The Florida Attorney General's Department of Legal Affairs issued 186 Notices of Alleged Violation to controllers under the Florida Digital Bill of Rights during the 2025 calendar year, with no penalties issued or collected, according to the authority's annual enforcement report published pursuant to s. 501.72(4), Florida Statutes.

On 10 February 2026, the Florida Attorney General's Office published its Florida Digital Bill of Rights Annual Enforcement Report covering the period from 1 January 2025 to 31 December 2025. According to the report, no financial penalties in any currency were issued or collected during the reporting period.

The Department of Legal Affairs reports receiving 1,496 consumer complaints and inquiries during the reporting period. Of these, 685 were closed as falling outside the scope of the Florida Digital Bill of Rights, while 811 were placed under active review as potentially falling within the categories of consumer relief set out under s. 501.705(2), Florida Statutes. The report notes that 130 complaints fell under two or more categories and were counted in each relevant category. The largest volume of complaints under active review related to consumer rights to confirm whether a controller is processing personal data and to access that data, accounting for 730 complaints. Further categories included deletion of personal data (97), correction of inaccuracies (74), portability (60), opting out of processing (53), opting out of collection of sensitive data including precise geolocation data (35), and opting out of collection via voice or facial recognition features (15).

On enforcement outcomes, the Department reports that 568 complaints were closed following initial review due to legal insufficiency, and 57 remain under initial review to determine what action, if any, is to be taken. The report states that 60 initial inquiries were made to potential controllers to assess whether they fall within the scope of enforcement under the Act (s. 501.72(4)(b), Fla. Stat.). Of the 186 Notices of Alleged Violation issued to controllers, the Department reports that 64 were resolved without litigation. According to the report, one active litigation is ongoing alleging violations of s. 501.701 et seq., Florida Statutes, against an alleged controller.
